天賦效果說明寫的是
「每擁有1層該喚醒體本回合下次狂氣爆發造成的傷害、力量和觸腕傷害提高、固定中毒、固定反擊、固定護盾、固定生命回覆、力量降低的最終效果提高1%。」
但進入戰鬥後卻可以發現該詞條與天賦的描述不同
限制了力量加成僅限於固定力量
這與不限制力量加成有什麼差別嗎？
當然有
就說一個最簡單理解的例子
沒限制的話戈利亞的狂氣爆發就能吃到這個加成
「獲得等同60%觸傷的臨時力量」
能吃到的話這個轉換比例最少也會為72%
所以這是標題詐欺。
花了抽數卻發現效果與描述不同
戈利亞錯失一碗湯

It even lets me put orison on them LOL

that's her thing
it's not a bug
oh
ig because the cards never entered my card they don't get the chance to disappear huh
no, its because they have 'destroy' keyword
it means they persist until actually used
sry, entered my hand
isn't that every card *without* the destroy keyword?

exhaust: disappears when used, never stays after the fight
destroy: disappears when used, stays after the fight if not used
neither: dosen't disappear, always stays after the fight

I think the problem is 
is it unintended action for "you can put orison on saya gerenerated   card copies"
Since the cards destroy themselves
the orisons don't persist long enough? idk
ok my bad, but isn't the difference between exhaust and destroy that destroy also removes the card when it gets discarded? It's not mentioned in game that it persists after the fight
nah that's just how orison works, you can put them on any command card without orison as long as they are in the deck (somehow), i saw someone put orison on GHelot's 4 cost

There are 3 different mechanism that 'temporary' cards use to remove themselves:
- Fleeting (Card disappears once your turn end,  or battle ends).
- Exhaust (Card disappears from your deck once played, or battle ends).
 - Destroy (Card that exists in your deck between battle, but get completely removed for the rest of the run once played in a battle).

You're mistaking destroy for fleeting.

the cards have Destroy *and* Fleeting, I don't think removing the card at end of battle is part of fleeting, it's just that fleeting and exhaust are usually paired. Although based on the description I think it might actually be that temporary cards are all removed unless they specifically have Destroy, since Destroy directly states 'stays in the deck after battle'

Hello. Are there any plans to add an option to filter out certain (un)desired awakeners on the record tab? It'd be nice to have that since some awakeners have very popular default team options (e.g., Castor and Pollux), but there are cases where someone wants to see alternatives to the popular composition. For example, I'm looking for Dtide comps that has Castor but not Pollux for some variety, but it's hard to do so since 80 to 90% of the clears have them together.
Oath-Bound Decree and Oathbound Oracle refer to the same thing but have a different name. Please pick whichever one you think sounds cooler 🙂

The description doesn't cover how the banner works. It should read something like this:

`Fated Soiree Triple Awakening Available! During the event, Keepers can randomly awaken 2 Assault SSR Awakeners and 3 recommended SSR WoDs for Assault Awakeners. These awakenings are tripled, for a total of 6 copies of Assault SSR Awakeners and 9 recommended WoDs.

Each Fated Soiree Triple Awakening event will close after 5 uses.`
The current language implies you draw 6 random Awakeners and 9 WoDs. This is not the case - you draw 5 times and each draw is then multiplied by 3.

yeah oe is pointless. I never use it on Vortice, used it on Arachne like once and Saya looks like the same type. I see what they want to do with exalts (stronger ults -> ult takes longer to get), but it does make me hesitant to oe characters. won't oe saya of sure

I think the point of high cost is that you have a stronger exalt but need a bit more time to charge, making you accumulate more aliemus as if they had 100 would be pointless in that sense.
They do need to make oe more appealing by really making it worth the extra high cost for it to compensate

OK, it’s fine that ultimates are their strong points, but the fact that they cost so much makes it, in my opinion, ridiculous to justify the OE. Honestly, in a alerta V , unless the character has their own frame or reload relics, it’s an impossible feat not to mention that they’re not at all appealing, in my view. I have my Vortice, Arachne and Saya at +12, and I recently played with them in the current D-tides; apart from Vortice, I didn’t find it appealing to try too use the OE for the other characters.

Yeah, that is what I say.
They need to make it appealing to use oe

I don't think they need to make it easier for them to make more aliemus, because then they can just use their normal exalt a lot faster in that case, defeating the purpose of making it 200 in the first place

Saya has a passive ability similar to other characters with a special Realm, however, something I don’t understand is why her main passive doesn’t scale with Sigil Yield to increase the propagation caro effects: what’s the point of giving her passive effects on E+ with Sigil Yield if the main passive, which is the core of her mechanics, only scales with Realm Mastery, besides the fact that this very passive is limited, and I don’t understand why they limit it if Arachne is a character who can literally charge the Prism of Singularity infinitely why limit Saya’s Embryos and give free rein to another character? especially since Saya’s buffs are limited to ultimates . It’s not like Arachne can´t enable insane bursts, healing, power, shield boosts everything essential with cards and still has no limits. It bothers me because what’s the point of upgrading this character to +12 if her core function doesn’t change at all whether it’s E0 or E12+ especially since her other power comes from the Soulforge Aptitude, meaning 40 stacks that could easily be in the main passive. They put them in an additional passive when Arachne already comes with all her upgrades and potential in her base kit it’s really ridiculous

It looks like someone made a bad investment 
I think it's pretty clear that characters like that aren't worth it +12 <:grin:1400771716665446471>

> why her main passive doesn’t scale with Sigil Yield to increase the propagation caro effects
you can revisit the last 6 character releases or so
scaling stat passives are part of character e1 effects now and have been for a while
they're no longer tied to a character's built in e0 passive
it's a dev decision

Yeah, I get that part. I think the passives on the E+s are okay. But what really bothers me are the stats on Caro’s Propagation passive: they scale purely off Realm Mastery, when Saya is a Black Yield stat character who barely gets any Realm Mastery. On top of that, the Embryos have a cap on their passive, and Caro’s Propagation passive effect is tied to Soulforge Aptitude.
oh so that's where the Arachne comparison comes from. Yeah, Saya passive (propagation caro) is kind of fool that only really shines in Arc 2 though to be fair, it still stacks a ton of corrosion
The point is why do they limit her realm passive so much compared to Arachne?
that is a realm passive, not a unit passive thecnically
so if other unit come out with the realm, they too will have to have the realm mastery passive
Arachne gets 10 Singularity Prism points just for doing a Pursuit. It's ridiculous to me that something like that isn't just baked into Saya's passive now.
<:Nymphaea_frustrated:1459078117485445288>
in a way, she's like GMurphy, the realm passive want RM (and max HP... kinda) but she herself wants Keyflare (or Crit), but unlike Sigil Yield, those stats r actually good and beneficial for the team
That's true, but I don't think it's fair to compare GMurphy to these other two characters, since she doesn't have a built in progressive buff upgrade system like Singularity Prism or Propagation.

I'm back for some more competitive nitpicking <:Alva_goodjob:1468259169965707398> 

Here, Aurita has 117 ATK, exactly 8 aliemus, and 34 crit rate. Her passive should give her ceil(117 * 0.01 * 34) + ceil(117 * 0.02 * ceil(8)) = 59 Tentacle Damage, but it ends up giving her 62, which happen to be how much the passive would give her if she had 9 Aliemus Regen. This pattern is consistent (9 Aliemus on tooltip, formula uses 10, etc 31 Aliemus on tooltip, formula uses 32 etc)

Is it intended ? Is it the result of hidden decimals ?

Also, since we're talking about Aequor, GDoll's Soulforge seems to be affecting Tentacle DMG, despite being "Base Damage" increase. No other source of Base Damage increase (Will Unyielding, Drowning in Crimson, Macabre Delicacies, etc) that I could think of affects Tentacle DMG. Is it intended ?

Aequor Symbiosis, the bonus to Tentacle DMG based on Max HP and the number of Chaos characters in a team, doesn't seem to be affected by other source of max HP like Birth of a Soul. Again, is it intended ?
蹲
想了一下解決方法，應該是在詞綴說明的最上面放一個現有的全部詞綴，下面再一個一個解釋比較合理
that might be the case

but it doesn't contradict what I said, Fleeting and Exhaust inherently have to contain that those cards disappear after battle ends (each) for the interaction with destroy to exist in the first place (that it hard retain the cards in battle and override that functionality)
For example there is "The Silver Key Dawn" from Hameln's wheel which should have both exhaust and destroy similarly to those cards you're mentioning  which have the fleeting and destroy keywords.
Fleeting and exhaust are the only keywords that let the game know "This card was generated during the battle"
exhaust does not remove the card from the deck after battle ends

exhaust cards generated mid-battle simply do not add to deck permanently because they're generated during the battle, and as a rule cards generated during a battle do not add to deck unless they have an explicit "stays in deck after battle" tag such as destroy
exhaust's only effect is to delete the card for the rest of that battle
you can test this via cards such at this that add exhaust-tagged cards to deck which reappear for every battle

Saya's duplicated cards are staying. 

So it opens 2 questions:

- is this the intended effect? 
- is having temporary cards generated during battle getting selected to obtain (and waste) orisons the intended effect?

1) yes. destroy is *specifically* tagged to stay after battle so that is the whole point, otherwise they would be exhaust tagged
2) that part is unclear, it might not be, we'll have to see if they change it

I thought the error was in adding destroy rather than exhaust. 

As it stands, Saya would be able to "farm" low or zero cost cards in weak fights and get to the boss with a gigantic deck full of cards that weren't made there. 

Unlike Aliemus, cards are an uncapped resource
yes
that's....kind of the entire point of her kit
yes
